title: Deferred Execution: JCL → JES
description: Submit a job and trace the execution chain. Identity binding, JES scheduling, deferred trust.

steps.title: What is JCL?
steps.expected: Understand the three parts of a JCL job: JOB, EXEC, DD.
steps.hint: JOB card = identity + accounting. EXEC = program to run. DD = data definition.

steps.title: Submit a Job via ISPF
steps.expected: Job submitted to JES input queue.
steps.hint: Type SUB on the command line and press Enter. Note the job number returned.

steps.title: JES Input Queue
steps.screen:
SDSF INPUT QUEUE

NJobname  JobID    Owner  Prty Queue  C Pos
-TESTJOB  JOB00123 HERC01 1    INPUT  A   1

Job is waiting for JES to schedule it.
steps.expected: Job appears in JES input queue with assigned job ID.
steps.hint: JES assigns a unique job ID. The job waits here until a class initiator picks it up.

steps.title: Job Execution
steps.screen:
SDSF STATUS DISPLAY

NJobname  JobID    Owner  Prty Status  C
-TESTJOB  JOB00123 HERC01 1    ACTIVE  A

Job is executing on an initiator.
steps.expected: Job moves from INPUT to ACTIVE status.
steps.hint: An initiator (address space) picks up the job and executes each step sequentially.

steps.title: Job Output (SYSOUT)
steps.screen:
SDSF OUTPUT QUEUE

NJobname  JobID    Owner  Prty  Queue Dest
-TESTJOB  JOB00123 HERC01 5     PRINT LOCAL

IEF142I TESTJOB STEP1 - STEP WAS EXECUTED - COND CODE 0000
steps.hint: Condition code 0000 = success. Non-zero codes indicate warnings or errors.

steps.title: Deferred Trust Implications
steps.screen:
Security Analysis: Deferred Execution

Key insight: JCL runs LATER under the submitter's identity.

Attack vector:
- Inject JCL into a trusted user's input queue
- Job runs with THEIR RACF identity
- Deferred = harder to detect in real-time

This is the mainframe equivalent of a cron job injection.
steps.expected: Understand why deferred execution creates unique security risks.
steps.hint: Unlike interactive commands, batch jobs run asynchronously — detection and attribution are harder.
